finding.summary: Excessive low-frequency content relative to highs, suggesting a dull or boomy snare sound.
finding.probable_cause: The source recording or snare tuning lacks high-frequency content; the measured 1/3-octave spectrum shows 100 Hz at -18 dBFS versus 4 kHz at -30 dBFS, a 12 dB difference indicative of a boxy/muffled tone.
finding.confidence_reason: The 1/3-octave spectrum is sparse (only three bands: 100 Hz, 1 kHz, 4 kHz), so the assessment of overall tonal balance is limited; no data above 4 kHz or below 100 Hz is available.

evidence_refs.description: 100 Hz band level at -18 dBFS, relatively high for a snare track
4 kHz band level at -30 dBFS, notably lower than the 100 Hz band

proposal.reason: The track has no FX chain, so no EQ or dynamics processing can be adjusted using the allowed operations (set_track_volume, set_track_pan, set_fx_param, set_fx_bypass). Track volume/pan cannot correct the spectral imbalance. No actionable move is possible from this diagnosis.
